lesson.menu_desc: Exchanging money & asking politely at the bank -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- Banking & Money Abroad
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Pricila Adamo | Aula 16 -- Banking & Money Abroad

listenings.text: Good afternoon, and welcome to City Exchange. Before you change your money, here is some useful information. Today's exchange rate is on the screen behind me, and it updates every hour. We charge a small fee of two percent on every transaction, so please check the final amount before you confirm. If you only need a little cash, the machine by the door is open all day, and it is contactless. For larger amounts, please come to the counter with your passport. Remember to keep your receipt -- you may need it at the airport. Thank you, and enjoy your trip.

When I first traveled abroad, money made me nervous. I never knew the exchange rate, and I was afraid of paying a huge fee. So now I follow a simple rule. Before I change anything, I ask three polite questions. Could you tell me what the rate is today? Do you know if there is a fee? And could you tell me where the nearest machine is? People are happy to answer when you ask politely. A calm question saves money, and it saves stress. Now I travel with a smile, and my wallet is happy too.

extra_audio.text: First, Pricila checks the exchange rate on the screen. Then she asks the teller if there is a fee. Next, she gives her passport and the amount she wants to change. After that, she counts the cash and keeps her receipt. Finally, she withdraws a little extra from the ATM by the door.
